content: This protocol draft has zero citations to test citation validation. Confidence level is moderate.

sections.body: The clinical rationale for this neurostimulation protocol is based on convergent evidence from quantitative EEG studies demonstrating frontal hypoactivation patterns in attention-deficit presentations.
This protocol is indicated for adults aged 18-65 years with diagnosed attention deficit conditions confirmed by comprehensive neuropsychological assessment.
Exclusion criteria include history of seizure disorder, intracranial metallic implants, and active substance use disorder within the past 6 months.
Absolute contraindications include active seizure disorder, intracranial metallic hardware, and implanted electrical devices.
Safety monitoring includes pre-session screening, continuous observation during stimulation, and post-session adverse event assessment.
Current intensity: 2 mA. Stimulation duration: 20 min. Session frequency: 3 per week. Total sessions: 15.
Primary outcome is change in theta/beta ratio at Fz measured by qEEG at baseline and post-treatment.
Pre-session vital signs, real-time impedance monitoring, post-session adverse event assessment.
Written informed consent required using approved consent document prior to enrollment.
Evidence supports frontal tDCS for attention conditions based on multiple trials with moderate effect sizes.
The evidence base is primarily derived from small-sample studies with limited long-term follow-up data.
